content.text: A GenAI portfolio without a sunset process is a portfolio with no off-ramp — use cases accumulate technical debt, consume platform capacity, and absorb governance attention long after they have stopped delivering value. Kill criteria and sunset rules give the CoE a principled mechanism for removing use cases from the portfolio, freeing platform resources and governance bandwidth for higher-value initiatives.

attrs.alt: Use-case lifecycle states and kill/sunset triggers

content.text: Why Kill Criteria Must Be Defined in Advance

Kill criteria are most useful when they are established at the point of intake approval — not when a struggling use case is already politically entrenched. A steering committee that approved a use case 18 months ago is reluctant to reverse that decision even when the evidence of underperformance is clear. Pre-committed kill criteria shift the burden: the question is no longer 'should we kill this?' (which feels like admitting failure) but 'has this use case triggered the criteria it committed to at launch?' (which is a factual question).
Establish kill criteria before approval, not after disappointment. A use case that accepted its kill criteria at launch cannot credibly argue that the criteria are unfair when they are triggered.

Kill criteria also prevent the portfolio from accumulating 'zombie' use cases — technically alive, consuming resources, but delivering no value. Zombie use cases are politically difficult to remove without explicit criteria because there is always a stakeholder who argues the use case might improve 'with a bit more time'.
Every use case in the portfolio moves through a defined lifecycle. The lifecycle has five states:
Use case is in production with active users; meeting performance targets
One or more trigger criteria have been hit; CoE is investigating
Investigation confirmed the trigger; remediation plan in place but not yet complete
Steering committee escalation from Under Review
Steering committee has decided to sunset; BU notified with 90-day deadline
Decision at At-Risk review; or planned sunset due to strategic change
System taken offline; data archived; users migrated; platform resources released
90-day notice period complete; all migration steps verified

Two tracks exist based on urgency. Immediate kill applies when a use case poses an active risk that cannot wait for a 90-day notice period. Planned sunset applies when the use case is underperforming or strategically misaligned but there is no active harm.

— System taken offline within 4 hours of decision. Triggers: active security incident, confirmed data breach traceable to the use case, CISO mandatory suspension order, active regulatory enforcement action. No BU notice period.

— 90-day notice to BU; structured decommission process with migration support from CoE. Triggers: performance underperformance, strategic misalignment, economic triggers, compliance issue that can be addressed by shutdown rather than emergency action.
Performance triggers are the most common reason a use case enters the Under Review state. They are based on metrics that were defined at launch in the use-case charter:
< 20% of target user population actively using the tool at 90 days post-launch
None — 90 days is the grace period
> 25% of AI outputs rejected/overridden by users for 30 consecutive days
< 30% of projected business impact at 6-month review
Zero active sessions for 60 consecutive days
Named BU sponsor formally withdraws endorsement in writing
Risk triggers are typically more urgent and may bypass the standard review cadence:
Immediate escalation to At-Risk; remediation deadline 30 days
May be immediate kill if incident is ongoing
Immediate kill if enforcement risk; planned sunset if advisory
Data breach traceable to use case
Immediate kill; forensic review before relaunch
Notify BU within 5 days of vendor notification; 90-day sunset unless replacement is available
BU owner dissolved or merged; no new BU owner identified within 30 days
Planned sunset unless surviving BU adopts it
Vendor solution now covers the use case
A commercially available product now solves the same problem at ≥80% quality and < 50% cost
Planned sunset of custom build; migrate to vendor product
Executive steering committee removes use case from funded portfolio
A fundamentally better technical approach makes the current implementation obsolete (e.g., a multimodal model supersedes a vision-specific pipeline)
Redesign if high value; sunset if low value

Kill criteria should be checked automatically against platform telemetry, not just reviewed at quarterly meetings. Automated checking surfaces issues earlier and prevents them from being overlooked in busy review cycles.

# Automated kill criteria evaluation
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import date, timedelta
from enum import Enum

class LifecycleState(Enum):
    ACTIVE         = "active"
    UNDER_REVIEW   = "under_review"
    AT_RISK        = "at_risk"
    SUNSET_NOTICE  = "sunset_notice"
    DECOMMISSIONED = "decommissioned"

class TriggerCategory(Enum):
    PERFORMANCE  = "performance"
    RISK         = "risk"
    STRATEGIC    = "strategic"

@dataclass
class KillTrigger:
    name: str
    category: TriggerCategory
    immediate_kill: bool = False    # True = offline within 4h; False = 90-day sunset track

@dataclass
class UseCaseMetrics:
    use_case_id: str
    launch_date: date
    target_users: int
    active_users_30d: int
    override_rate_30d: float        # fraction (0.0–1.0)
    measured_impact_usd: float
    projected_impact_usd: float
    days_zero_usage: int            # consecutive zero-usage days
    open_p1_incidents: int
    days_since_oldest_p1: int | None
    ciso_suspension: bool
    vendor_api_active: bool
    bu_sponsor_active: bool
    cost_overrun_multiple: float    # actual / projected cost

    def evaluate_triggers(self) -> list[KillTrigger]:
        triggers = []
        as_of = date.today()
        days_since_launch = (as_of - self.launch_date).days

        # Performance: adoption
        if days_since_launch >= 90:
            adoption_rate = self.active_users_30d / max(self.target_users, 1)
            if adoption_rate < 0.20:
                triggers.append(KillTrigger("Low adoption (<20% of target at 90d)", TriggerCategory.PERFORMANCE))

        # Performance: override rate
        if self.override_rate_30d > 0.25:
            triggers.append(KillTrigger("High override rate (>25% for 30d)", TriggerCategory.PERFORMANCE))

        # Performance: measured impact
        if days_since_launch >= 180:
            impact_ratio = self.measured_impact_usd / max(self.projected_impact_usd, 1)
            if impact_ratio < 0.30:
                triggers.append(KillTrigger("Low impact (<30% of projection at 6m)", TriggerCategory.PERFORMANCE))

        # Performance: zero usage
        if self.days_zero_usage >= 60:
            triggers.append(KillTrigger("Usage abandonment (60+ consecutive days)", TriggerCategory.PERFORMANCE))

        # Performance: sponsor withdrawal
        if not self.bu_sponsor_active:
            triggers.append(KillTrigger("BU sponsor withdrawal", TriggerCategory.PERFORMANCE))

        # Risk: unresolved P1
        if self.open_p1_incidents > 0 and self.days_since_oldest_p1 and self.days_since_oldest_p1 > 14:
            triggers.append(KillTrigger("Unresolved P1 incident >14 days", TriggerCategory.RISK))

        # Risk: CISO suspension (immediate kill)
        if self.ciso_suspension:
            triggers.append(KillTrigger("CISO mandatory suspension", TriggerCategory.RISK, immediate_kill=True))

        # Risk: vendor API gone (immediate dependency)
        if not self.vendor_api_active:
            triggers.append(KillTrigger("Vendor API discontinued", TriggerCategory.RISK))

        # Economic: cost overrun
        if self.cost_overrun_multiple > 3.0:
            triggers.append(KillTrigger(f"Cost overrun ({self.cost_overrun_multiple:.1f}x projected)", TriggerCategory.STRATEGIC))

        return triggers

    def recommend_action(self) -> dict:
        triggers = self.evaluate_triggers()
        if not triggers:
            return {"state": LifecycleState.ACTIVE.value, "triggers": [], "action": "No action required"}

        immediate = [t for t in triggers if t.immediate_kill]
        if immediate:
            return {
                "state": LifecycleState.DECOMMISSIONED.value,
                "triggers": [t.name for t in immediate],
                "action": "IMMEDIATE KILL: Take offline within 4 hours. Notify BU and CISO.",
            }

        return {
            "state": LifecycleState.UNDER_REVIEW.value,
            "triggers": [t.name for t in triggers],
            "action": "Enter Under Review state. CoE Lead investigation within 5 business days.",
        }

# Example evaluation
metrics = UseCaseMetrics(
    use_case_id="uc-007",
    launch_date=date(2025, 1, 15),
    target_users=200,
    active_users_30d=28,              # 14% adoption — below 20% threshold
    override_rate_30d=0.12,
    measured_impact_usd=65_000,
    projected_impact_usd=480_000,     # 13.5% of projection — well below 30%
    days_zero_usage=0,
    open_p1_incidents=0,
    days_since_oldest_p1=None,
    ciso_suspension=False,
    vendor_api_active=True,
    bu_sponsor_active=True,
    cost_overrun_multiple=1.4,
)

result = metrics.recommend_action()
print(f"Use case: {metrics.use_case_id}")
print(f"Recommended state: {result['state']}")
print(f"Action: {result['action']}")
if result['triggers']:
    print("Triggers fired:")
    for t in result['triggers']:
        print(f"  • {t}")
# Use case: uc-007
# Recommended state: under_review
# Action: Enter Under Review state. CoE Lead investigation within 5 business days.
# Triggers fired:
#   • Low adoption (<20% of target at 90d)
#   • Low impact (<30% of projection at 6m)

A planned sunset is a structured project, not an event. The 90-day notice period exists to give BU stakeholders time to migrate users, extract data, and close integrations. A poorly managed sunset creates exactly the kind of political damage that makes future sunset decisions harder to make.
Sunset decision communicated to BU leadership and users
User data export completed and handed to BU
Exported user data in standard format; BU sign-off
BU identifies replacement workflow or tool; CoE advisory support
Users migrated to replacement; monitoring for migration completeness
Integration dependencies confirmed closed (API keys revoked, webhooks removed)
System taken offline in staging; final verification
System decommissioned; resources released; final audit
Decommission audit report; archived to programme records
BU teams frequently push back on sunset decisions, particularly for use cases they championed. The CoE should have a documented escalation path for disputes:

— BU can formally dispute the sunset decision by submitting a remediation plan that addresses each triggered criterion. The CoE Lead reviews within 5 business days. If the remediation plan is credible, the use case re-enters the At-Risk state with a 60-day remediation deadline.
— If the BU disagrees with the CoE Lead's decision on the remediation plan, they can appeal to the steering committee within 14 days of the CoE Lead's decision. Steering committee decision is final.

— A use case that has been through the full At-Risk → sunset process and re-enters under the same triggers cannot be re-saved by a second remediation plan. The second time a use case triggers the same criteria, sunset is automatic.

A sunset use case is a learning opportunity, not just a failure. The programme should systematically extract insights from every planned sunset to improve intake scoring, kill criteria calibration, and delivery practices for future use cases.

# Sunset post-mortem template
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import date

@dataclass
class SunsetPostMortem:
    use_case_id: str
    title: str
    launch_date: date
    sunset_date: date
    triggers_fired: list[str]
    root_causes: list[str]
    intake_score_at_launch: dict          # original value/risk scores
    what_worked: list[str]
    what_failed: list[str]
    process_improvements: list[str]       # changes to intake scoring, kill criteria, delivery

    def months_in_production(self) -> float:
        return (self.sunset_date - self.launch_date).days / 30

    def render_summary(self) -> str:
        lines = [
            f"SUNSET POST-MORTEM: {self.use_case_id} — {self.title}",
            f"Duration: {self.months_in_production():.1f} months in production",
            f"Triggers: {', '.join(self.triggers_fired)}",
            "",
            "Root causes:",
        ]
        for rc in self.root_causes:
            lines.append(f"  • {rc}")
        lines.append("")
        lines.append("Process improvements for future use cases:")
        for pi in self.process_improvements:
            lines.append(f"  ✓ {pi}")
        return "\n".join(lines)

# Example post-mortem
pm = SunsetPostMortem(
    use_case_id="uc-007",
    title="Finance Report Drafting Assistant",
    launch_date=date(2025, 1, 15),
    sunset_date=date(2025, 9, 1),
    triggers_fired=["Low adoption (<20% at 90d)", "Low impact (<30% at 6m)"],
    root_causes=[
        "Target users (finance analysts) were not involved in requirements definition — tool did not match their workflow",
        "Value projection assumed 100% adoption by month 3; historical comparable tools in this BU achieved 40% adoption at 12 months",
        "No change management plan; tool launched without training or user onboarding sessions",
    ],
    intake_score_at_launch={"value": 3, "risk": 1},
    what_worked=[
        "Platform stability was excellent (zero platform-level incidents)",
        "Guardrails performed correctly — no PII leakage events",
    ],
    what_failed=[
        "Requirements not validated with target users before build",
        "Adoption projections not grounded in BU-specific adoption history",
        "No change management plan; no training; no onboarding",
    ],
    process_improvements=[
        "Add mandatory target user validation session to Tier 1 intake requirements",
        "Require BU to submit adoption comparison to 3 previous tool launches in the same BU",
        "Require change management plan (training, comms, onboarding) as a launch gate criterion",
    ],
)
print(pm.render_summary())

A healthy portfolio has a non-zero kill rate. If no use cases are ever killed, the kill criteria are either too lenient or not being enforced. The steering committee should review the portfolio kill rate as a governance health indicator:
Kill criteria too lenient, or governance not enforcing them; portfolio accumulating zombies
Audit all active use cases against kill criteria; enforce existing criteria before adding new ones
Healthy — portfolio is being actively managed; kill criteria are enforced but not so aggressive they discourage innovation
No action required; maintain quarterly reviews
Aggressive but potentially appropriate for a programme in early Phase 2–3 where pilot learning is high
Review whether kills are due to genuine failure or over-aggressive criteria; adjust intake scoring if too many high-potential use cases are being killed prematurely
Portfolio may be taking on too many high-risk use cases, or kill criteria are being applied inconsistently
Review intake scoring model; check for criteria being applied retroactively rather than at the criteria defined at launch

Kill criteria must be defined at intake approval — not when a struggling use case is already politically entrenched. Pre-committed criteria shift the decision from 'admitting failure' to 'evaluating facts'
Use-case lifecycle has five states: Active, Under Review, At Risk, Sunset Notice, Decommissioned. Each state has defined entry conditions and review cadence
Two tracks: immediate kill (offline within 4 hours for active security/regulatory incidents) and planned sunset (90-day notice for performance, strategic, and economic triggers)
Performance triggers: < 20% adoption at 90 days, > 25% override rate, < 30% of projected impact at 6 months, 60+ days zero usage, BU sponsor withdrawal
Risk triggers: unresolved P1 > 14 days, CISO mandatory suspension, data breach, vendor API discontinued
The 90-day sunset process is a structured project with weekly milestones; BU has 10 days to submit a remediation plan or appeal to the steering committee
Systematically extract post-mortem learnings from every sunset to improve intake scoring, kill criteria calibration, and delivery practices
A healthy portfolio kill rate is 1–5% annually; 0% indicates the portfolio is accumulating zombies that governance is not enforcing criteria against
